audit.method: Static inspection: README sections, code blocks, badges, screenshots, LICENSE/CONTRIBUTING, openapi/swagger, shell completions, devcontainer, Taskfile/justfile/Makefile, anyhow/thiserror usage, a11y markers in web sources, governance files.

criteria.readme: install steps, usage example, demo/screenshot, contributing link, license badge
criteria.onboarding: presence of install + quickstart + first-run example in <5min
criteria.cli_help: presence of CLI surface + --help quality + completions
criteria.ax: aria-/role= attributes + axe-core tests + focus/keyboard guidance

repos_not_found: phenotype-shared (no top-level directory; interpreted as 'phenoShared' per existing repo convention)

results.notes: Rust workspace + iOS. 00_START_HERE.md is exemplary: status table, prereqs, copy-paste commands, dev-trap warning re: PhenoObservability path-dep. focus-cli seeded-demo flow is a real onboarding shortcut. No completions, no OpenAPI (not a service), no AX (mobile-iOS scope).
top_improvements: Add cargo-dist or clap_complete to focus-cli to ship zsh/bash/fish completions alongside binaries.
Add a devcontainer.json so contributors do not need to hand-clone PhenoObservability for the path-dep trap (00_START_HERE.md already warns about it).
Ship a `focus doctor --json` preflight subcommand to detect missing prereqs and report them with actionable hints.

results.notes: Infra/IaC monorepo. README is operator-grade: Day-1 numbered steps, OCI primary bring-up, runner registration, ADR list, status snapshot. Taskfile.yml present. Errors are Rust-bench-grade (anyhow/thiserror). No CLI surface for end-users.
top_improvements: Add a `task bootstrap` wrapper that pre-checks terraform/opentofu/AWS/OCI config and reports per-check failure context (matches existing Day-1 numbered steps).
Add an ADR-driven 'Operational status' badge to the README header so newcomers see health at a glance (currently buried in H2).
Add a CONTRIBUTING quick-link to the README 'Contribution rules' section header for discoverability.

results.notes: Shared Rust dev-tools crate (worktree-manager, dag-scheduler, fr-trace, etc). README short and focused: install (single + all), verify, adoption in other repos, governance. Devcontainer + justfile + Taskfile.yml all present (rare complete set). Multi-binary CLI surface needs generated completions + umbrella discoverability.
top_improvements: Add a top-level `tooling` umbrella binary that prints `tooling list` / `tooling run <name>` discoverability across 10+ sub-bins (today users must know each crate).
Wire clap_complete into each binary to generate per-tool completions, and ship a `tooling completions install` to drop them in ~/.zsh/completions.
Add a `docs/reference/cli/` index page auto-generated from clap_mangen so docs stay in sync with binary surface.

results.notes: Shared cross-cutting library (phenotype-nanovms-client + others). README is structured (Crates / Quick Start / Usage / Architecture / Consolidation opportunities) but lacks a 'when to use this' consumer table and end-to-end runnable snippet. Errors are anyhow-grade. No dev UX surface (library).
top_improvements: Add a per-crate mini-README and an `examples/` directory; today 'Usage Examples' section is generic and a copy-paste from quick-start.
Publish docs.rs badges and a generated rustdoc link in the README header so SDK consumers can land on the API reference in one click.
Add a 'When to use this' table mapping each crate to its sibling repos that consume it (focalpoint, OmniRoute, thegent), reducing hand-roll duplication.

results.notes: Niche: journey manifest + vhs-tape schema. README is exemplary for a metadata repo: numbered Quickstart, ground-truth assertions, schema link, repo layout, license. Good ratio of badges (9) to signal strength. Dev surface is one CLI (phenotype-journey); errors moderate.
top_improvements: Run cargo-dist on bin/phenotype-journey to publish GitHub release binaries + shell completions in one workflow.
Add a sample stub manifest under examples/ that consumers can `phenotype-journey validate examples/sample.json` against, so step 3 of the quickstart is copy-pasteable today.
Embed an example vhs tape output (animated GIF or screenshot) in the README so the 'Recording a CLI tape' step is visual, not just textual.

results.notes: Astro+Bun monorepo of 8 product landing sites (agileplus, byteport, hwledger, phenokits, projects, thegent, odin). README is operator-grade (subtree, lockfile, CI). 122 a11y hits across sites is healthy for a greenfield monorepo. No CLI surface. Per-site DX is determined by Astro scaffolds; no shared component lib across sites is a missed unification.
top_improvements: Add CONTRIBUTING.md (currently missing) so per-site contributors have one canonical place for brand tokens, accessibility contract, and subtree rules.
Add a top-level `pnpm sites <name>` wrapper plus a Playwright + axe-core a11y gate in CI; AX hits exist but there's no enforcement.
Promote `packages/ui` and `packages/design-tokens` to be the single source-of-truth for hero/CTA components across all 8 sites (currently each site has its own).

results.notes: Agent runtime/CLI + system bootstrap. README is the gold standard for this batch: 2450 lines, install/quickstart/dispatch/CLI commands/dotfiles/templates all covered. Has OpenAPI reference (the only one in batch besides OmniRoute). Dev tooling is full (just + Taskfile + devcontainer). thegent-dispatch CLI has examples in README but no published completions.
top_improvements: Promote docs/reference/api/openapi_api.md to a real openapi.{yaml,json} and add a Redoc/Swagger UI page under docs-site/ so consumers can explore the schema.
Wire clap_complete into thegent-dispatch and thegent-bootstrap, ship completions via cargo-dist.
Add a 'first 60 seconds' animated GIF showing thegent bootstrap -> dispatch -> result; the 2450-line README still requires a lot of scrolling before the wow moment.

results.notes: Consumer-facing AI gateway. README is marketing-grade: 108 code blocks, 35 badges, 50+ screenshots, 21 i18n translations, dashboard preview, install/quickstart/Docker/Desktop sections. Has real openapi.yaml + openapi-coverage and openapi-security-tiers tests. Web dashboard is the AX surface; no completions for the gateway CLI; error msgs are TS-code (not Rust-grade).
top_improvements: Add an accessibility (axe-core) test in CI against the Electron + web dashboard; marketing is great but dashboard AX is unverified.
Ship a typed SDK generator (openapi-typescript or @omniroute/sdk) referenced in the README so consumers do not hand-roll fetch wrappers.
Add a 'fail-loud' error envelope contract: every 4xx/5xx returns `{code, message, hint, docsUrl}` so the SDK can show actionable errors (today's TS errors are catch-all).
Add a Makefile or `npm run` task suite so contributors do not have to discover `pnpm` scripts across @omniroute/* subpackages.

common_strengths: Every repo with a CLI uses Rust + anyhow/thiserror, giving consistent error wrapping.
License + Contributing present in 7/8 repos; phenotype-landing is the only gap.
Taskfile.yml is the de-facto DX wrapper in 6/8 repos; phenotype-tooling and thegent also have justfile (good redundancy).
OmniRoute is the only consumer-grade artifact (i18n, marketing, openapi-coverage test). Other repos are operator/internal.
common_gaps: 0/8 repos ship generated shell completions. clap_complete is a 5-line addition per binary and is the single highest-leverage DX win in the batch.
0/8 repos have observability/UX-metrics hooks (telemetry, feature-flag dashboards, command-success rates).
0/8 repos have a CONTRIBUTING that links to a 'good first issue' / triage board; the on-ramp for new contributors is unlabeled.
2/8 repos have no devcontainer (focalpoint, phenotype-landing); focalpoint especially hurts because of the PhenoObservability path-dep trap.
0/8 repos have a 'quick demo' (<5min) video/GIF; 5/8 are operator-readme-style only.
prioritized_l4_initiatives: DX-1: Add clap_complete + cargo-dist to every Rust CLI binary (focalpoint focus-cli, phenotype-tooling umbrellas, phenotype-journey, thegent-dispatch).
DX-2: Add a CONTRIBUTING.md + good-first-issue labels batch across all 8 repos; gate on a CONTRIBUTING presence check.
DX-3: Add devcontainer.json + AX pre-commit (axe-core) to phenotype-landing and focalpoint.
DX-4: Promote OmniRoute's openapi.yaml + openapi-coverage test to be the template that thegent and any other service-bearing repo adopts.

summary.best_ax: phenotype-landing (122 a11y hits) and thegent (dedicated tests/a11y/)
summary.best_readme: thegent (2450 lines) and OmniRoute (108 code blocks, 21 i18n)
summary.best_onboarding: focalpoint (00_START_HERE.md) and thegent (numbered install + dispatch + bootstrap)
